title: Kadai Paneer
tagline: Paneer and peppers in a freshly-ground kadai masala gravy.
macroNotes: Most of the calories and fat are from the full-fat paneer and the cream. To lighten it: use 200 g paneer instead of 250 g, skip the 1 tbsp cream, and cook the gravy in 1 tbsp oil instead of oil + ghee — saves ~120 kcal and ~12 g fat per serving.
Already high protein from the paneer (~26 g/serving). For an even bigger hit, stir in 40 g soaked, squeezed, chopped soya chunks with the gravy, or swap in low-fat paneer.

ingredients.group: Homemade kadai masala (makes a little extra)

items: 1 tbsp of the homemade kadai masala

steps.title: Roast the kadai masala
steps.body: Dry roast 1.5 tsp coriander seeds + 2 tsp cumin seeds + 4-5 Kashmiri red chillies + 1.5 tbsp peppercorns + 1 tbsp salt in a pan over medium heat for 3-5 minutes, stirring constantly, until you get a nutty aroma and the spices darken slightly. Don't let them burn.

steps.title: Grind it
steps.body: Let the roasted spices cool completely, then grind to a fine powder. You'll use 1 tbsp here; store the rest in a jar.

steps.title: Start the gravy
steps.body: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan over medium heat. Add 1 tsp cumin seeds and 1 inch chopped ginger, sauté until fragrant.

steps.title: Cook the onions
steps.body: Add 2 large chopped onions and 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste. Sauté until the raw smell goes and the onions turn soft and golden.

steps.title: Bloom the spices
steps.body: Add 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp degi chilli powder and 1 tsp coriander powder. Sauté for about 1 minute until the spices smell cooked.

steps.title: Simmer the gravy
steps.body: Add the puree of 2 large tomatoes and salt to taste, plus a splash of water. Let it cook down until the oil starts to separate, about 6-8 minutes. Set this gravy aside.

steps.title: Sear the peppers
steps.body: In a separate wide pan, heat 1 tsp ghee + 1 tsp oil. Add 1 sliced onion, 1/2 sliced capsicum and 1 sliced tomato with a pinch of salt. Sauté on high for a minute so they stay slightly crisp.

steps.title: Add the paneer
steps.body: Add 250 g sliced paneer and sauté gently for a minute so it takes on colour without going rubbery.

steps.title: Coat with masala
steps.body: Sprinkle in 1 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder and 1 tbsp of the homemade kadai masala. Toss well so the paneer and peppers are coated.

steps.title: Bring it together
steps.body: Pour the prepared gravy into the paneer pan and stir well to combine. Simmer 2-3 minutes.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Stir in 1 tbsp cream (if using), mix, and garnish with a coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or naan.

notes: The homemade kadai masala is the whole point here — don't substitute a shop-bought garam masala. The freshly-roasted peppercorns and Kashmiri chilli are what make it taste like a restaurant kadai.
Keep the peppers and paneer slightly under-cooked and crisp; they finish cooking in the gravy.
Scale is linear: to make a full 1 kg batch of paneer, multiply everything by 4.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
